vocab.def: passive past — used to say who created something
passive past — used to say who found something first
passive past — used to say who constructed something
passive past — used to say who created or improved something
passive past — used to say who wrote something
passive past — used to say who designed something
an ongoing action that was stopped by another event
past continuous — action in progress at a past time

blanks.l2: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
The Eiffel Tower was designed by Gustave Eiffel.
Hamlet was written by William Shakespeare.
The theory of relativity was developed by Albert Einstein.

s: watching TV when the zombies attacked.
blanks.l2: I was watching TV when the zombies attacked.

s: playing football when the lightning struck.
blanks.l2: They were playing football when the lightning struck.

s: a book when the robot appeared.
blanks.l2: She was reading a book when the robot appeared.

order.l2: The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming.
The theory of relativity was developed by Albert Einstein.
Romeo and Juliet was written by Shakespeare.
I was sleeping when the zombies attacked.
She was watching TV when the aliens invaded.
He was fighting the aliens when a meteor shower destroyed his spaceship.
The makeup artist was doing my makeup when I sneezed.

freewrite.prompt: Rewrite in the passive: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web.
freewrite.broken: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web.
a: The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee.
freewrite.explanation: Passive: object (The World Wide Web) + was + past participle (invented) + by + agent.

freewrite.prompt: Rewrite in the passive: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin.
a: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming.
freewrite.explanation: Passive: Penicillin (object) + was discovered (past passive) + by Alexander Fleming.

freewrite.prompt: Rewrite in the passive: The Wright Brothers invented the airplane.
freewrite.broken: The Wright Brothers invented the airplane.
a: The airplane was invented by the Wright Brothers.
The airplane was invented by The Wright Brothers.
freewrite.explanation: Object first: The airplane + was invented + by the Wright Brothers.

freewrite.prompt: Rewrite in the passive: Gustave Eiffel designed the Eiffel Tower.
freewrite.broken: Gustave Eiffel designed the Eiffel Tower.
a: The Eiffel Tower was designed by Gustave Eiffel.
freewrite.explanation: Passive: The Eiffel Tower + was designed + by Gustave Eiffel.

freewrite.prompt: Rewrite in the passive: Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity.
freewrite.broken: Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity.
a: The theory of relativity was developed by Albert Einstein.
freewrite.explanation: Passive: The theory of relativity + was developed + by Albert Einstein.

freewrite.prompt: Use past continuous to say what you were doing when a zombie attacked. Start with 'I was...'
a: I was sleeping when a zombie attacked.
I was eating dinner when a zombie attacked.
I was watching TV when a zombie attacked.
I was studying when a zombie attacked.
I was running when a zombie attacked.
freewrite.explanation: Past continuous: I was + verb-ing + when + simple past (the interrupting event).

repair.broken: The telephone was invent by Alexander Graham Bell.

errors.explanation: Passive requires the past participle: invented (not the base form invent).

a: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell.

repair.broken: Radiation was discovered by Marie Curie. She were a very brave scientist.
a: Radiation was discovered by Marie Curie. She was a very brave scientist.

repair.broken: I were watching TV when the aliens invaded.

errors.explanation: I (first person singular) takes 'was' in past continuous, not 'were'.

a: I was watching TV when the aliens invaded.

repair.broken: She was run when the volcano erupted.

errors.explanation: Past continuous needs was + verb-ING: was running (not was run).

a: She was running when the volcano erupted.

repair.broken: Romeo and Juliet were written by Shakespeare.

errors.explanation: Romeo and Juliet (as a single title/work) is singular: was written.

a: Romeo and Juliet was written by Shakespeare.
